label: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 6

extraction.one_line_summary: KUSS Sepp attacked from a 40-rider breakaway on the Javalambre climb, won by 26 seconds over MARTINEZ Lenny who took the red jersey at age 20.
tactical_patterns: JUMBO VISMA planted KUSS, VALTER, VAN BAARLE in 40-rider break, splitting GC defense burden while embedding stage win threat.
MARTINEZ Lenny at 17 seconds deficit pre-stage rode BARDET's wheel strategically to minimize work before taking leader's jersey.
ROGLIČ Primož aided by VINGEGAARD Jonas on chase reduced EVENEPOEL's time gap significantly despite 40-rider break going clear.
Break formation so violent it crashed VINE Jay out; peloton smaller than breakaway reveals miscalculation by RED jersey group.

rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: Won from 40-rider break while teammates ROGLIČ and VINGEGAARD attacked peloton behind, triple tactical gain for JUMBO VISMA.
rider_signals.MARTINEZ Lenny: positive: At 20 became youngest grand tour leader since 1904, conserved energy on BARDET's wheel despite reported friction.
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: Lost significant time to ROGLIČ and VINGEGAARD despite holding ninth overall, willingness to surrender jersey suggests tactical retreat.
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: positive: Third place finish on Javalambre despite reported friction with MARTINEZ shows climbing form restored after illness concerns.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Attacked from chasing group with VINGEGAARD support to reclaim time on EVENEPOEL, gap now debris versus substantial deficit.
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: neutral: Paced ROGLIČ on Javalambre chase despite no GC ambitions, confirms pure domestique role for JUMBO VISMA at Vuelta.
rider_signals.VINE Jay: negative: Crashed out during violent break formation battle, illness also cited as contributing factor to abandonment.
